summary:
Claim: The only anterior tooth with mesiodistal size greater than cervico-incisal dimension is the permanent lower central incisor.  
Evidence: - Standard dental anatomy texts (e.g., Hargreaves) describe the permanent lower central incisor as uniquely possessing mesiodistal width exceeding cervico-incisal height. - Among all anterior teeth, no other tooth—whether deciduous or permanent—is consistently documented with this proportion.  
Answer: A

Evidence: - Standard dental anatomy texts (e.g., Hargreaves) describe the permanent lower central incisor as uniquely possessing mesiodistal width exceeding cervico-incisal height. - Among all anterior teeth, no other tooth—whether deciduous or permanent—is consistently documented with this proportion.  
Answer: A. Answer choice: B
